entries.definition:
ʿ–r–r scabies; dirt, dung; shame, to disgrace, to be exposed; to
be of bad character; to harm; needy, to seek hospitality, to seek
alms. Of this root, two words occur once each in the Qur’an: مَعَرَّة
maʿarratun and المُعْتَرّ ʾal-muʿtarr.

مَعَرَّة maʿarratun [n.] that which disgraces, disgrace, shame;
guilt (48:25) فتصيبكم منهم معرة and there befall you guilt on their
account.

مُعْتَرّ muʿtarr [act. part. of v.VIII اعتَرّ iʿtarra] hospitality
seeker; alms seeker, beggar (22:36) وأطعموا القانع والمعتر and feed

611
